sn46.4:1.1: On one occasion Ven. Sāriputta was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
sn46.4:1.4: “Yes, friend,” the monks responded to him.
sn46.4:2.1: “Friends, there are these seven factors for awakening.
sn46.4:2.3: Mindfulness as a factor for awakening, analysis of qualities as a factor for awakening, persistence as a factor for awakening, rapture as a factor for awakening, calm as a factor for awakening, concentration as a factor for awakening, & equanimity as a factor for awakening.
sn46.4:2.4: These are the seven factors for awakening.
sn46.4:2.5: “Whichever factor for awakening among these seven factors for awakening I want to dwell in during the morning, I dwell in that factor for awakening during the morning.
sn46.4:2.6: Whichever factor for awakening I want to dwell in during the middle of the day, I dwell in that factor for awakening during the middle of the day.
sn46.4:2.7: Whichever factor for awakening I want to dwell in during the late afternoon, I dwell in that factor for awakening during the late afternoon.
sn46.4:2.8: “If the thought occurs to me, ‘mindfulness as a factor for awakening,’ the thought occurs to me, ‘It is immeasurable’; the thought occurs to me, ‘It is well-mastered.’ While it remains, I discern, ‘It remains.’
sn46.4:2.9: If it falls away from me, I discern, ‘It has fallen away from me because of this condition.’ [Similarly with analysis of qualities as a factor for awakening, persistence as a factor for awakening, rapture as a factor for awakening, calm as a factor for awakening, and concentration as a factor for awakening.]
sn46.4:2.10: “If the thought occurs to me, ‘equanimity as a factor for awakening,’ the thought occurs to me, ‘It is immeasurable’; the thought occurs to me, ‘It is well-mastered.’ While it remains, I discern, ‘It remains.’
sn46.4:2.11: If it falls away from me, I discern, ‘It has fallen away from me because of this condition.’
sn46.4:3.1: “Suppose, friends, that a king or king’s minister had a wardrobe full of many-colored clothes.
sn46.4:3.2: Whichever set of clothes he might want to wear during the morning, he would wear that set of clothes during the morning.
sn46.4:3.3: Whichever set of clothes he might want to wear during the middle of the day, he would wear that set of clothes during the middle of the day.
sn46.4:3.4: Whichever set of clothes he might want to wear during the late afternoon, he would wear that set of clothes during the late afternoon.
sn46.4:3.5: “In the same way, whichever factor for awakening among these seven factors for awakening I want to dwell in during the morning, I dwell in that factor for awakening during the morning.
sn46.4:3.6: Whichever factor for awakening I want to dwell in during the middle of the day, I dwell in that factor for awakening during the middle of the day.
sn46.4:3.7: Whichever factor for awakening I want to dwell in during the late afternoon, I dwell in that factor for awakening during the late afternoon.
sn46.4:3.8: “If the thought occurs to me, ‘mindfulness as a factor for awakening,’ the thought occurs to me, ‘It is immeasurable’; the thought occurs to me, ‘It is well-mastered.’ While it remains, I discern, ‘It remains.’
sn46.4:3.9: If it falls away from me, I discern, ‘It has fallen away from me because of this condition.’ [Similarly with analysis of qualities as a factor for awakening, persistence as a factor for awakening, rapture as a factor for awakening, calm as a factor for awakening, and concentration as a factor for awakening.]
sn46.4:3.10: “If the thought occurs to me, ‘equanimity as a factor for awakening,’ the thought occurs to me, ‘It is immeasurable’; the thought occurs to me, ‘It is well-mastered.’ While it remains, I discern, ‘It remains.’
sn46.4:3.11: If it falls away from me, I discern, ‘It has fallen away from me because of this condition.’”
